title: Pellaea nivea
taxonomy: Pellaea nivea is classified within the family Pteridaceae. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Pellaea nivea is further classified as a member of the genus Pellaea, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The species nivea is the specific epithet for this plant, indicating its unique characteristics. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, Pellaea nivea is one of the vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Pellaea nivea, also known as the silver fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate and silver-white fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pellaea nivea include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pellaea nivea is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, with the most common preparations including a tea made from the dried fronds and a cold-pressed oil extracted from the plant's rhizome.
